title: Mexican Smoky Chiles
description: A flavorful dish with a smoky twist, perfect for a quick lunch. Chiles add a spicy kick to this easy recipe.

ingredients.name: Mexican chiles
ingredients.notes: You can use any type of mild chiles you prefer

ingredients.name: Piquant sauce
ingredients.notes: You can adjust the amount to your desired level of spiciness

ingredients.name: Smoky chipotle peppers
ingredients.notes: Use 1 if you prefer a milder flavor, 2 for a spicier taste

ingredients.name: Grill
ingredients.notes: You'll need a grill to cook the chiles

instructions: Preheat a grill to medium heat.
Place the chiles on the grill and cook for 2-3 minutes per side, or until they're slightly charred.
Transfer the chiles to a bowl and let them cool down.
Once the chiles are cool enough to handle, peel off the skin, remove the seeds, and chop them into small pieces.
In a pan, heat the piquant sauce over medium heat.
Add the chopped chiles and smoky chipotle peppers to the pan and stir well.
Let the mixture simmer for 2-3 minutes, or until it thickens slightly.
Serve the Mexican Smoky Chiles warm, garnished with chopped fresh cilantro if desired.
